title: Ship Systems Outfitting
entry_mechanism: Saudi construction and industrial companies upgraded to naval standards through PSAP Track 3
executive_summary:
Ship systems outfitting — the installation of electrical, HVAC, piping, damage control, and habitability systems aboard naval vessels — represents 15–25% of the total cost of a naval shipbuilding programme. Saudi Arabia's Avante 2200 corvette programme, being built in Spain by Navantia with planned Saudi industrial participation, creates an immediate and contractually anchored demand for Saudi outfitting work packages. The outfitting trades — electrical installation, pipework, HVAC, insulation, and joinery — are directly adjacent to Saudi Arabia's existing construction and industrial services capability.

The global naval outfitting market is estimated at $3.2 billion annually. Saudi Arabia's naval expansion programme — including the Avante 2200 corvettes and future surface combatants — creates a decade-long pipeline of outfitting work packages. Saudi Arabia's existing construction industry, which executes complex MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing) works on industrial and offshore platforms at scale, requires primarily a standards and quality upgrade, not a capability build from scratch.

Phase I outfitting capability establishes Saudi Arabia's first naval industrial footprint and creates the workforce foundation for the more complex Phase II naval manufacturing nodes — combat systems integration and radar maintenance.

companies.name: Navantia
companies.rationale: Navantia is the Avante 2200 prime contractor and holds all work package allocation authority. Engage through SAMI's Navantia programme office to negotiate outfitting work packages for Saudi companies.
companies.contact: Navantia VP Saudi Arabia Programme → Navantia VP Industrial Cooperation

companies.name: Thales Naval
companies.rationale: Thales supplies combat management and naval systems and has strong Gulf naval relationships. Thales can support Saudi companies in understanding naval-grade electrical and systems installation requirements.

companies.name: Kaefer (maritime insulation)
companies.rationale: Kaefer is Europe's leading naval insulation and outfitting specialist with a track record of establishing international naval outfitting operations. A Kaefer technical partnership gives Saudi companies access to Lloyd's Register-compliant outfitting procedures.

companies.name: Fincantieri
companies.rationale: Fincantieri is Europe's largest naval shipbuilder and has active Gulf naval partnerships. Fincantieri's outfitting expertise and quality systems are directly transferable to Saudi companies.

companies.name: Damen Shipyards
companies.rationale: Damen builds naval and offshore vessels and has active Middle East partnerships. Damen's modular shipbuilding approach is amenable to outsourced outfitting work packages.

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Industrial Services (AMSCO)
partners.current: Complex MEP works on offshore platforms; piping, electrical, HVAC for industrial facilities
partners.upgrade: Qualify to Lloyd's Register naval classification for piping and electrical installation; establish dedicated naval outfitting team; Navantia technical supervision for first Avante 2200 work packages

partners.name: Saudi Binladin Group / Saudi Arabia Contracting
partners.current: Large-scale MEP contracting; electrical installation; HVAC systems
partners.upgrade: Naval-grade electrical installation standards; Lloyd's Register supplier qualification; dedicated naval outfitting division

partners.name: Almabani General Contractors
partners.current: Industrial construction; MEP for petrochemical and infrastructure
partners.upgrade: Naval systems installation qualification; Lloyd's Register approval for piping and HVAC; Kaefer technical partnership for insulation

partners.name: TVTC Maritime Training Centre
partners.current: Maritime training programmes; some naval maintenance training
partners.upgrade: Naval outfitting trades programme; Lloyd's Register certified training; placement with Saudi outfitting companies on Avante 2200 programme
